event: Shard rebalance started, relocating a subset of gateway sessions across node groups
Gateway disconnects and identify traffic rose sharply, causing widespread client reconnects
Early hypothesis focused on regional edge instability because disconnects were global and user-visible
Redis and auth latency rose as secondary symptoms, creating a competing hypothesis of session-store saturation
Resume rejection telemetry showed failures concentrated on sessions touched during shard transfer windows
Team concluded reconnect amplification was primary and approved rollback of the gateway release
Resume rejection ratio, identify rate, and downstream saturation all began returning to baseline
